zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Burr於1517年出生在英國亨廷格多史爾格亞特 格藍斯登時，她的父親Richard Burr22歲，母親Agnes DeBishops17歲。她於大約1538年與William Seeley在英國亨廷格多史爾格亞特 格藍斯登結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1560年9月6日在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾格亞特 格藍斯登享年43歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾格亞特 格藍斯登。

nameMeanings.name: Burr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English burre ‘bur’ (a seed-case or flower-head with clinging prickles), used by Shakespeare to denote someone who sticks like a bur, a person difficult to ‘shake off’, a sense which may well be older.
German: topographic name from Burr(e) ‘mound, hill’, or in the south a variant of Burrer .

History: The American political leader Aaron Burr (1756–1836) was the son of a clergyman and academic, president of Princeton University. On his mother's side he was descended from the Puritan preacher Jonathan Edwards; on his father's from Jehu Burr, who emigrated from England to MA with John Winthrop (see   Winthrop  ) in 1630.

variants.name: Burrer
meanings: from an agent derivative of Middle High German burren ‘to buzz, complain’, probably applied as a nickname for a noisy or cantankerous person.
habitational name for someone from either of two places called Burren in Württemberg.

variants.name: Burrs
meanings: Altered form of English Burr , with the addition of excrescent -s, or perhaps of Burrows .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Burr was born in 1517, in Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, England, her father, Richard Burr, was 22 and her mother, Agnes DeBishops, was 17. She married William Seeley about 1538, in Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 6 September 1560, in Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Burr est née en 1517, à Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Burr, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Agnes DeBishops, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé William Seeley environ 1538, à Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 6 septembre 1560, à Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterrée à Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Burrが1517年にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアグレアト グランスデンで生まれた時，父Richard Burrは22歳，母Agnes DeBishopsは17歳であった。約1538年にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアグレアト グランスデンでWilliam Seeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1560年9月6日にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアグレアト グランスデンで43歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアグレアト グランスデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Burr(이)가 1517년 영국 헌틴그도실르 그리트 그랜즈든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Burr(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes DeBishops(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1538년 영국 헌틴그도실르 그리트 그랜즈든에서 William Seeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1560년 9월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 그리트 그랜즈든에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 그리트 그랜즈든에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com William Seeley aproximadamente 1538, em Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Burr родилась в 1517 г. в местности грит грансден, хантингдоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Burr было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes DeBishops, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Seeley около 1538 г. в местности грит грансден, хантингдоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 6 сентября 1560 г. в местности грит грансден, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и была похоронена в местности грит грансден, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
